name: Buddhist Cosmology
connections: Samsara - the cycle of existence binding all realms
Nirvana - escape from the six realms
Bodhisattva realms - enlightened beings aiding others
Pure Lands - celestial realms of buddhas
description: The six realms of existence (Sanskrit: gati or bhava) are the destinations for rebirth in Buddhist cosmology, depicted in the Bhavachakra (Wheel of Becoming). These realms are driven by karma—the consequences of intentional actions—and the three root poisons: greed, hatred, and ignorance.

richMetadata.structure: Six realms arranged in a wheel (Bhavachakra) representing different states of rebirth. Organized hierarchically from heavenly realms (gods/demi-gods) through human and animal realms to hellish and hungry-ghost realms. Each realm populated according to karma.
connections: Samsara - the cycle of existence binding all realms
Nirvana - escape from the six realms
Bodhisattva realms - enlightened beings aiding others
Pure Lands - celestial realms of buddhas
richMetadata.significance: The six realms illustrate the consequences of karma and the Three Poisons (greed, hatred, ignorance). Fundamental to Buddhist understanding of suffering and the path to enlightenment. All beings cycle through realms according to their actions and mental state.
parallels: Hindu cosmology - multiple realms of existence
Pythagorean transmigration - soul movement between states
Christian judgment - souls assigned to levels
sources: Tibetan Book of the Dead (Bardo Thodol)

keyMyths.title: The Story of King Bimbisara and the Hungry Ghosts
keyMyths.description: King Bimbisara, in a past life, neglected to offer food to enlightened monks. As a result, he was reborn as a hungry ghost (preta) and tormented by insatiable hunger, highlighting the karmic consequences of actions and the importance of generosity.

keyMyths.title: Devadatta's Fall from Grace
keyMyths.description: Devadatta, a cousin of the Buddha, attempted to usurp his position and even tried to harm him. His actions, driven by jealousy and hatred, led to his descent into the lowest hell realm (Naraka), illustrating the severe consequences of negative karma.

keyMyths.title: The Brahma's Pride
keyMyths.description: Beings in the Brahma realms, characterized by immense bliss and long lifespans, often develop the mistaken belief that they are eternal creators. The Buddha's teachings challenge this notion, emphasizing the impermanence of all existence, even in the highest realms.
keyMyths.source: Brahmajala Sutta (The Net of Perfect Wisdom)

keyMyths.title: The Founding of Jetavana Monastery
keyMyths.description: Anathapindika, a wealthy merchant, purchased land covered in gold coins to build the Jetavana monastery for the Buddha and his followers. This act of immense generosity demonstrated the path to accumulating positive karma and contributing to the spread of Dharma.

keyMyths.title: Moggallana's Journey Through the Realms
keyMyths.description: Moggallana, one of the Buddha's chief disciples, possessed great psychic powers and was known for visiting the various realms of existence. He witnessed the suffering and joy experienced by beings in each realm, reinforcing the cyclical nature of samsara and the need for liberation.

sources.name: Abhidharmakośabhāṣya
sources.description: A key text of Abhidharma, written by Vasubandhu, detailing the structure and contents of the Buddhist cosmos.
sources.title: Abhidharmakośabhāṣya

sources.name: Visuddhimagga (The Path of Purification)
sources.description: A comprehensive manual of Buddhist practice by Buddhaghosa, including descriptions of the different realms of existence and the meditative states leading to them.
sources.title: Visuddhimagga (The Path of Purification)

sources.name: Bhavacakra (Wheel of Becoming) depictions and commentaries
sources.description: Visual representations and associated texts explaining the cyclical nature of existence and the six realms.
sources.title: Bhavacakra (Wheel of Becoming) depictions and commentaries

sources.name: The Princeton Dictionary of Buddhism
sources.description: A comprehensive dictionary providing definitions and explanations of key Buddhist concepts, including cosmology.
sources.title: The Princeton Dictionary of Buddhism

sources.name: Early Buddhist Texts Society translations
sources.description: Translations of Pali Canon texts (Nikayas) containing many suttas that describe the six realms and related concepts.
sources.title: Early Buddhist Texts Society translations
